text:
Market today: Geopolitical tensions are heating up with US strikes on Iran and rising crude prices. This creates a risk-off mood, which typically hurts your growth-heavy tech and chip names. Plus, there's specific chatter about chip stocks losing their shine.

Your holdings: SOXL is a major concern, down -35.06% and it's a leveraged, decaying ETF. SPAL, another leveraged ETF, is also down significantly at -23.1%. QQQI, your income anchor, is at -2.16% and nearing its stop. Remember, margin amplifies these drops.

Watch: Keep a close eye on geopolitical developments and how crude prices react. Also, watch for any further weakness in the broader chip sector.

Bottom line: Focus on managing risk in your leveraged and underperforming positions, especially with current sector headwinds.
plays: SOXL: Down -35.06% and leveraged. Seriously consider a substantial trim or tightening your stop far below its current 200 to protect capital.
SPAL: This leveraged ETF is down -23.1%. Review its default stop at 21.38; these decay and move fast.
QQQI: With a -2.16% loss and a stop at 52, respect that level. Consider trimming if it approaches the stop.
